content:
Here is the weekly ASX X thread for the week of 29 June 2026:

Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,412 points. The dominant theme is end-of-financial-year positioning. View is cautious — quarter-end rebalancing flows can distort price action and mask underlying conviction. Watch volume carefully. #ASX #ausbiz #investing

Post 2
Resources and financials are the two sectors to watch. Resources face month-end index reweighting pressure. Financials see EOFY book-squaring from super funds deploying fresh mandates. Both face technically significant flow events. #ASX #banking #mining #ausbiz

Post 3
BHP is the stock to watch. As the largest ASX constituent by weight, any shift in iron ore pricing around USD 98 per tonne feeds directly into index-level moves and sets the tone for the broader resources tape. #ASX #ironore #mining #ausbiz

Post 4
S&P 500 closed at 5,611 points. US markets are digesting tariff uncertainty ahead of the July 9 deadline. If Wall Street softens on trade risk, ASX exporters and materials names absorb the first hit at Monday open. #ASX #ausbiz #globalmarkets #equities

Post 5
RBA minutes drop Tuesday 1 July. Market pricing sits near 25bps of cuts by August. A hawkish tone crushes rate-sensitive REITs and banks. A dovish lean lifts yield proxies hard into EOFY. The asymmetry favours the downside surprise. #ASX #RBA #ausbiz #interestrates

Post 1
ASX 200 +59.20pts, +0.68% to 8,823.40 — crossing above its 200-day MA. Tech led the charge as growth names caught a bid. Index sits 4.12% below its 52-week high. #ASX #ausbiz #equities

Post 2
IT surged +4.04%, Health Care +2.12%. Utilities sank -2.58%, A-REITs -0.96%. Rate-sensitive defensives sold off as growth sectors absorbed the day's risk appetite. #ASX #ausbiz #tech

Post 3
NEU +36.07% to AUD16.60 dominated the session. A single-stock catalyst drove the move, making it the standout of the day across the entire ASX 200. #ASX #ausbiz #healthcare #biotech

Post 4
Gold at AUD5,906.17/oz, Silver AUD85.27/oz. AUD/USD at 0.6888. Precious metals firm in local terms, supporting materials +0.85% despite gold miners selling off. #ASX #gold #silver #commodities

Post 5
Watch 8,823 — the ASX 200 just crossed its 200-day MA. A close below that level this week would signal a failed breakout. US data overnight is the key trigger to monitor. #ASX #ausbiz #technicalanalysis
